info.name: InsightXpert API
info.description:
API collection for InsightXpert — AI data analyst for Indian digital payment transactions.

Base URL: http://localhost:8000

request.description: Returns server status and timestamp. Use this to verify the server is running.

Send a natural language question and receive Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream.

Chunk types in the stream:
- `status` — progress update (e.g., "Searching knowledge base...")
- `tool_call` — agent is calling a tool (run_sql, get_schema, search_similar)
- `sql` — the SQL query being executed
- `tool_result` — result from tool execution
- `answer` — final natural language answer
- `error` — error message

Stream ends with `data: [DONE]`.

**Note:** Postman doesn't render SSE well. Use curl for best results:
```
curl -N -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/chat \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"message":"What is the average transaction amount?"}'
```

response.name: 200 SSE Stream

request.description:
Send a question and get all response chunks at once (non-streaming).

Returns a JSON object with a `chunks` array containing all the same chunk types as the SSE endpoint, but collected into a single response.

Better for Postman testing since it returns normal JSON.

response.name: 200 OK

item.name: Chat — Example Questions
item.description: Pre-built requests for each of the 6 query categories in the challenge.

item.name: Comparative — Avg amount by txn type

request.description: Returns the full DDL (CREATE TABLE statements) and list of table names from the connected database.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Add a question-SQL pair to the RAG knowledge base. The agent uses these as few-shot examples for future queries.

`type` can be: `qa_pair`, `ddl`, or `documentation`.
Add business documentation to the RAG store. This context is injected into the agent's system prompt when relevant.
Add DDL schema information to the RAG store.

Delete all RAG embeddings from all ChromaDB collections (qa_pairs, ddl, docs, findings).

This is a destructive operation — the RAG knowledge base will be empty after this call. The server will re-bootstrap training data on next restart.

Requires authentication.

item.name: Ollama
item.description:
Manage Ollama models — pull (download), list, and delete local models.

Requires Ollama to be running on the server (default: http://localhost:11434).

request.description:
Download the Ministral 3B model from the Ollama registry. Streams progress via SSE.

SSE events include:
- `{"status": "pulling manifest"}` — starting download
- `{"status": "downloading ...", "completed": 1234, "total": 5678, "percent": 21.7}` — download progress
- `{"status": "success", "model": "ministral:3b"}` — download complete
- `[DONE]` — stream end

**Note:** Use curl for best SSE experience:
```
curl -N -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/ollama/pull \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"model":"ministral:3b"}'
```

Download the Llama 3.2 1B model. Streams progress via SSE.

Llama 3.2 comes in 1B and 3B variants (no 2B). Use `llama3.2:3b` for the larger version.
Download the Llama 3.2 3B model. Streams progress via SSE.
Pull any Ollama model by name. Replace the model value with any model from the Ollama registry (https://ollama.com/library).
List all Ollama models that are currently downloaded on the server.

Delete a locally downloaded Ollama model to free disk space.

Replace `ministral:3b` in the URL with the model name to delete.
